title: Hungary’s upheaval signals a wider revolt against the politics of permanence
standfirst: Viktor Orbán’s defeat by Péter Magyar is more than a domestic upset: it is a rebuke to a style of power that has fused national identity, patronage and media control into one durable machine. The result will be felt well beyond Budapest, from Brussels and Kyiv to Moscow, where an EU sceptic who doubled as a Kremlin interlocutor has been abruptly replaced by an uncertain but emphatically pro-European challenger.
